abstract:
Large Language Models (LLMs) trained on massive code corpora have significantly advanced the field of code intelligence. However, current models often face two critical limitations: they are typically constrained to a single architecture (such as encoder-only or decoder-only) that may not be optimal for all applications, and they rely on a narrow set of pretraining objectives that may not generalize well across diverse downstream tasks. To overcome these hurdles, we introduce CodeT5+, a versatile family of encoder-decoder LLMs designed for code. CodeT5+ features a modular design that allows its components to be flexibly reconfigured to suit the specific requirements of various tasks.

This architectural flexibility is supported by a diverse mixture of pretraining objectives, including span denoising, contrastive learning, text-code matching, and causal language modeling, applied to both unimodal and bimodal multilingual datasets. To scale effectively without the prohibitive cost of training from scratch, we initialize CodeT5+ using frozen, off-the-shelf LLMs and further employ instruction-tuning to enhance alignment with human intent. We evaluated CodeT5+ across more than 20 benchmarks in zero-shot, fine-tuning, and instruction-tuning settings. Our results demonstrate state-of-the-art performance across a wide spectrum of tasks. Notably, our instruction-tuned CodeT5+ 16B model achieves a new state-of-the-art on the HumanEval code generation benchmark with 35.0% pass@1 and 54.5% pass@10, outperforming other open-source code LLMs and even surpassing OpenAI's code-cushman-001.
